name: Docker Image Tag Generator

on:
  push:
    branches: ["*"]
    tags: ["v*"]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      branch_name:
        description: "Branch name override"
        required: false
        default: ""
      commit_sha:
        description: "Commit SHA override"
        required: false
        default: ""
      tag:
        description: "Git tag override"
        required: false
        default: ""
      pr_number:
        description: "PR number override"
        required: false
        default: ""

permissions:
  contents: read

env:
  # Defaults that can be overridden by workflow_dispatch inputs
  INPUT_BRANCH_NAME: ${{ github.event.inputs.branch_name || '' }}
  INPUT_COMMIT_SHA: ${{ github.event.inputs.commit_sha || '' }}
  INPUT_TAG: ${{ github.event.inputs.tag || '' }}
  INPUT_PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.inputs.pr_number || '' }}

jobs:
  generate-tags:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install PowerShell
        run: |
          if ! command -v pwsh &> /dev/null; then
            apt-get update && apt-get install -y wget apt-transport-https software-properties-common
            wget -q "https://packages.microsoft.com/config/ubuntu/22.04/packages-microsoft-prod.deb"
            dpkg -i packages-microsoft-prod.deb
            apt-get update && apt-get install -y powershell
          fi
          pwsh --version

      - name: Detect git context
        id: git-context
        run: |
          # Use workflow_dispatch inputs if provided, otherwise detect from git
          if [ -n "$INPUT_BRANCH_NAME" ]; then
            BRANCH="$INPUT_BRANCH_NAME"
          else
            BRANCH="${GITHUB_HEAD_REF:-${GITHUB_REF#refs/heads/}}"
          fi

          if [ -n "$INPUT_COMMIT_SHA" ]; then
            SHA="$INPUT_COMMIT_SHA"
          else
            SHA="${GITHUB_SHA:-$(git rev-parse HEAD)}"
          fi

          if [ -n "$INPUT_TAG" ]; then
            TAG="$INPUT_TAG"
          elif [[ "$GITHUB_REF" == refs/tags/* ]]; then
            TAG="${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/}"
          else
            TAG=""
          fi

          if [ -n "$INPUT_PR_NUMBER" ]; then
            PR="$INPUT_PR_NUMBER"
          else
            PR="${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
          fi

          echo "branch=$BRANCH" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "sha=$SHA" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "tag=$TAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "pr=$PR" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

          echo "Detected context:"
          echo "  Branch: $BRANCH"
          echo "  SHA: $SHA"
          echo "  Tag: $TAG"
          echo "  PR: $PR"

      - name: Generate Docker image tags
        id: generate-tags
        run: |
          pwsh -File docker-image-tag-generator.ps1 \
            -BranchName "${{ steps.git-context.outputs.branch }}" \
            -CommitSha "${{ steps.git-context.outputs.sha }}" \
            -Tag "${{ steps.git-context.outputs.tag }}" \
            -PrNumber "${{ steps.git-context.outputs.pr }}"

      - name: Run Pester tests
        run: |
          pwsh -Command "Install-Module -Name Pester -MinimumVersion 5.0 -Force -Scope CurrentUser -SkipPublisherCheck; Invoke-Pester -Path ./docker-image-tag-generator.Tests.ps1 -Output Detailed -CI"

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env pwsh
<#
.SYNOPSIS
    Test harness that runs all Docker image tag generator tests through act (GitHub Actions).
    Each test case sets up a temp git repo, runs act, captures output, and asserts results.

.DESCRIPTION
    This script:
    1. Validates the workflow YAML structure
    2. Validates actionlint passes
    3. For each test case: creates a temp git repo with fixtures, runs act, parses output
    4. Saves all output to act-result.txt
    5. Asserts exact expected values in act output
#>

$passedTests = 0
$failedTests = @()

        $script:failedTests += "[$($tc.Name)] Exception: $($_.Exception.Message)"
    }

    }

Write-Result "=" * 70
Write-Result "TEST SUMMARY"
Write-Result "=" * 70
Write-Result "Total: $totalTests | Passed: $passedTests | Failed: $($failedTests.Count)"

    foreach ($ft in $failedTests) {

    }

}
else {
